examples: Neither Philippa Fawcett, who in 1890 scored higher than any man in the mathematics tripos, nor Elsie Phare, who gained a starred first in English in the 1920s, gained degrees.
The process, mostly run by Smallholders in the farm and environment ministries and the special Phare office, had been, he said, a near-catastrophe.
The Commission will withdraw its accreditation to two payment agencies, one in the finance ministry and another in the regional development ministry, which were handling Phare funds.
Leur pays semblait brusquement redevenir le phare du monde arabe.
